issues.title: CI runs the real-DB regression suites and deploys gate on green
issues.summary: CI sets neither DATABASE_URL nor MANAGED_DATABASE_URL, so every real-DB incident-regression suite is silently skipped under a green badge; deploys are not gated on tests. Until this lands, no fix below is actually verified by the suite. Also: makemigrations --check, .dockerignore.

findings.title: CI sets neither DATABASE_URL nor MANAGED_DATABASE_URL, so every real-DB incident-regression suite is skipped under a green badge
findings.chain: ci.yml env block (no DATABASE_URL/MANAGED_DATABASE_URL) -> module/class skipif guards in listed suites evaluate true -> pytest skips -> CI green without executing any incident regression
findings.claim: Extends the known CI-skip finding: .github/workflows/ci.yml test-job env defines DATABASE_USER/PASSWORD/TEST_DATABASE_NAME/HOST/PORT but not DATABASE_URL, so even suites with a DATABASE_URL fallback skip. CI-dead set: test_view_schema_builder.py (788-LOC truncation-incident regressions), test_ocs_materializer.py, TestConnectPageReplayIdempotency (the f26c1a0/2587158 dedup pin with realistic id-less payloads — it exists and is well-built but never executes in CI), TestWriteCases/TestWriteForms, and TestExecuteAsyncIntegration (SET ROLE/search_path/statement_timeout). A green CI badge certifies none of the 'is the incident actually fixed' tests.

findings.title: CI never runs the 2026-06-10 incident regression tests or any real-DB writer/role-isolation tests (module-level MANAGED_DATABASE_URL skip)
findings.claim: tests/test_view_schema_builder.py, test_ocs_materializer.py, and the TestConnectIdlessWriters / CommCare writer tests in test_materializer.py carry module-level pytestmark skipif(not MANAGED_DATABASE_URL/DATABASE_URL); no GitHub workflow sets those vars (test.py documents CI sets neither), so all of them skip — including the #227 truncation-collision regressions, the four fully-mocked #229 last_error-surfacing regressions that need no DB, the f26c1a0 duplication and 2587158 missing-id regressions, and the role-isolation grant tests. tenant isolation (SET ROLE) is only tested against a MagicMock asserting 'SET ROLE' strings. The regressions written for the production incidents have never executed in CI; locally they fall back to DATABASE_URL and run DROP SCHEMA CASCADE against the dev DB.

findings.title: Deploys not gated on tests; migration ordering by convention; labs migrations opt-in default-off; version-skew windows
findings.claim: ci.yml runs only on pull_request; deploy.yml triggers on push to main with no CI dependency, so direct pushes/admin-merges/jointly-broken PR pairs deploy untested, and CI lacks makemigrations --check. Migrations run at API-container boot, but deploy order is MCP -> API -> Worker, so new MCP code runs against the old schema until the API boots and the old worker runs against the new schema until step 3, with no documented rollback for a mid-pipeline failure; DEPLOYMENT.md claims a pre-deploy migration hook but .kamal/hooks/pre-deploy is 'exit 0'. deploy-labs.yml's run_migrations input defaults to false, so deploying a backend image with model changes while unchecked ships code against an unmigrated DB with nothing detecting it.

findings.title: No .dockerignore: documented manual-deploy path bakes developer .env/.env.deploy into the production image; base.py loads /app/.env at runtime
findings.claim: Dockerfile does COPY . . with no .dockerignore, and DEPLOYMENT.md documents local 'kamal setup/deploy' as a supported path — a dev checkout's .env (real API keys, Fernet key), .env.deploy (RDS endpoint/ARNs), .git, node_modules etc. are copied into ECR image layers. base.py:24-26 reads /app/.env in every container; read_env doesn't overwrite Kamal-injected vars, but any var the Kamal env blocks omit (SOCIALACCOUNT_ALLOWED_EMAIL_DOMAINS, DEFAULT_LLM_MODEL, CONNECT_API_URL, OCS_URL, AGENT_RESUME_*) is silently sourced from whoever's laptop built the image. CI builds are clean.

issues.title: Real (unmocked) chat<->MCP contract test + frontend test infra
issues.summary: Stand up FastMCP in-process with a real client, no mocks, on the highest-churn seam. Would have caught the recipe breakage, toolCallId mismatch, get_metadata card bug, and onboarding 404 as a CLASS. Includes frontend unit-test infra and retiring the mocks that pin dead seams.

findings.title: Chat-to-MCP wire has zero unmocked coverage; tests mock the exact seams that hid production bugs
findings.claim: No test starts the FastMCP server and connects the real mcp_client to it: test_mcp_client patches MultiServerMCPClient, test_mcp_chat_integration mocks get_mcp_tools with hand-written fakes whose schemas can drift from server.py, and test_mcp_server/test_mcp_tenant_tools call handlers in-process with the DB mocked at the ORM/psycopg boundary. The highest-churn, highest-fix-density seam (server.py, graph/base.py, chat/views.py) is the one seam the suite cannot observe — the same masking pattern that hid the recipes signature drift (build_agent_graph patched), the refresh data-loss (run_pipeline patched), and a prior SynchronousOnlyOperation bug (aresolve_credential patched). MCP_TOOL_NAMES membership is a cross-process contract enforced only by hand, and its test asserts the constant against itself; the dbt-execution path has zero non-mocked coverage.

findings.title: Frontend has no unit-test infrastructure; e2e runs nowhere; post-deploy smoke tests exist but are wired to nothing
findings.claim: frontend/package.json has no test runner — only Playwright e2e scripts that never run in CI (the frontend job is ESLint only) and partly require live credentials or interactive mode; the threadId-leak incident fix (00c423d, useWorkspaceThreadSync.ts) and workspace switching have no regression coverage, and 13k LOC of hand-written TS API-shape mirrors are verified only by tsc against themselves (the same disease that produced the onboarding 404 and phantom is_public types). tests/smoke/test_deployment.py and test_connect_sync.py exist but are excluded by default addopts ('-m not smoke') and no deploy workflow runs any pytest/playwright step — both June incidents were detected by humans noticing stuck UI, while an automated post-deploy smoke run is already written and never invoked.

findings.title: Test suite is structurally blind to the known incident classes: mocks pin the exact dead seams (FutureApp current_app, oauth_tokens, FAILED-vs-CANCELLED, stale-cursor selection, RUNNING false-failure), affirms the pg_catalog disclosure as contract, and tests no role enforcement
findings.chain: Per-bug seam mocking: _make_stale_pending_job only builds PENDING; tests patch jobs_cancel.current_app; tests use nonexistent tenant_membership.tenant.external_id; resume test asserts oauth_tokens; sql_validator pins pg_catalog/public allowed; cancel test asserts only data.cancelled; stale-cursor test injects prior_run via mocked filter chain; merge suite no TenantMetadata; OCS tests happy-path only; trigger test mocks pipeline instantly
findings.claim: A systematic mock audit shows the suite cannot detect the known production bugs because tests mock or pin the exact failing seams: (1) reconcile_stale_thread_job's RUNNING false-failure branch is never constructed — every stale-reconcile test builds PENDING via _make_stale_pending_job and _procrastinate_job_status is an AsyncMock, so the known false-failure ships unguarded. (2) jobs_cancel tests patch apps.workspaces.api.jobs_cancel.current_app, the same module-level FutureApp import-time binding that is a known surviving sibling site, so the binding failure can't fire and cancellation-reaches-Procrastinate is unverified. (3) MCP tool tests set mock_run.tenant_schema.tenant_membership.tenant.external_id, a path that doesn't exist on the real model (production reads run.tenant_schema.tenant.external_id), passing only because MagicMock fabricates attributes — proving contract-drift blindness. (4) Resume test asserts 'oauth_tokens' in agent config, codifying the dead OAuth plumbing as contract. (5) sql_validator's TestPromptValidatorAlignment pins unqualified pg_catalog views and public.* as ALLOWED, certifying the known cross-tenant disclosure vector. (6) cancel_materialization test asserts only data.cancelled True, never the written state, so it passes before/after a FAILED-vs-CANCELLED fix. (7) stale-cursor regression injects prior_run via the mocked filter/exclude/order_by chain, so the real prior-run selection query is untested and the two-registries finding is uncatchable. (8) merge-users suite has zero TenantMetadata assertions, so the cascade-delete data-loss path is invisible. (9) OCS loader tests are happy-path MagicMock only (no 401/403/malformed/retry), mirroring the Connect-only hardening asymmetry at the test layer. (10) transformation trigger test mocks run_transformation_pipeline to return instantly, hiding synchronous in-request dbt execution and the dbt-superuser class.

findings.title: Prompt instructs run_materialization with a pipeline= parameter the tool no longer accepts (single-tenant branch); recurring prompt-vs-contract drift
findings.claim: The single-tenant schema-context prompt tells the agent to call run_materialization with pipeline='<name>', but the MCP tool signature has no pipeline parameter (routing moved into materialize_workspace per-provider) and all real params are hidden, so the LLM-facing schema has zero properties for it. The multi-tenant prompt builder correctly omits the argument. Depending on FastMCP arg handling the first materialization call in a fresh single-tenant workspace either errors and retries (feeding the escalation counter) or silently drops the arg. Recurring prompt/validator drift class (#190, 93504d5).

findings.title: Context-injection contract relies on both LangChain and FastMCP skipping arg validation
findings.claim: The injecting node adds workspace_id/user_id/thread_id/tool_call_id to every MCP tool call including tools that declare none of them; this works only because LangChain's BaseTool._parse_input returns dict-schema input unvalidated and FastMCP's ArgModelBase has pydantic-v2 default extra='ignore'. If either library tightens, every MCP tool call fails at once. The same silent-drop also masks the live prompt drift instructing run_materialization with a pipeline= arg the tool dropped.

issues.title: One identifier helper: 63-byte + collision guard on every minted name
issues.summary: The #227 fix was applied to view names only. Build ONE helper (length/collision/sanitization, keyed by (provider, external_id)) that schema, role, refresh, dbt model/alias names all route through. Closes the cross-tenant collision class -- same bug family as the 2026-06-10 incident.

findings.title: Tenant identity keyed by bare external_id: cross-provider / punctuation collision routes one tenant into another's schema
findings.chain: apps/users/services/tenant_resolution.py:99-103,156-159 numeric external_ids -> mcp_server/context.py:56-59 filter(tenant__external_id=...) no provider -> apps/workspaces/services/schema_manager.py:625-631 sanitize collides -> schema_manager.py:66-77 provision matches by schema_name only, returns other tenant's row -> mcp_server/services/materializer.py:183-184 loads into returned schema; also schema_manager.py:306 Tenant.objects.get(external_id=) MultipleObjectsReturned
findings.claim: Tenant is unique on (provider, external_id), but provision() resolves and returns an existing TenantSchema by schema_name ALONE (no tenant FK check), and _sanitize_schema_name maps distinct external_ids to one name (Connect opp '123' and OCS experiment '123' both -> 't_123'; 'a-b'/'a_b'/'a.b' collide). On collision a tenant gets the other tenant's live ACTIVE schema and the materializer DROP/CREATEs its tables there — simultaneous cross-tenant data destruction and disclosure; load_tenant_context (filter on tenant__external_id with no provider predicate) then serves the co-mingled schema for both. build_view_schema additionally does Tenant.objects.get(external_id=...) which raises MultipleObjectsReturned if any cross-provider duplicate exists, breaking multi-tenant builds.

findings.title: Tenant schema names and derived role/refresh names have no 63-byte guard (truncation fix applied to view names only)
findings.claim: PR #227/#2aaf4fb added byte-length/collision guards for view names (_view_prefix capped at 32 + sha256 digest, build_view_schema hard-fails >63 bytes), but the sibling site _sanitize_schema_name emits unbounded schema names from external_id (Tenant.external_id is CharField max 255). PostgreSQL silently truncates identifiers to 63 bytes, so two long external_ids sharing a 63-byte prefix get distinct TenantSchema rows but one physical schema (CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS finds the first) — cross-tenant data co-residence/clobbering invisible to Django, parameterized information_schema lookups using the full string match nothing (empty catalog). readonly_role_name (+'_ro') and create_refresh_schema (+'_r{8hex}') truncate differently, yielding a truncated _ro role shared across tenants (cross-tenant read primitive). Live exposure is CommCare domains (free-text); Connect/OCS ids are short today.

findings.title: dbt model names and column aliases from CommCare metadata have no 63-byte guard; synchronous /runs/trigger/ blocks the request thread with a per-process lock
findings.claim: commcare_staging builds dbt model names (stg_case_{slug}, {parent}__repeat_{group_slug}) and column aliases from provider-controlled metadata; slugify bounds shape not length, TransformationAsset.name is CharField(255) with a shape-only regex, and dbt materializes each as a relation truncated by Postgres to 63 bytes — two long form/case names sharing the first ~63 bytes produce two asset rows but one physical relation (dbt builds one over the other, ref() ambiguous), and _unique_alias disambiguates in Python before truncation so long property names still collide. Separately, TransformationRunViewSet.trigger is a synchronous DRF action running dbt inline under a process-global threading.Lock, blocking the serving thread for the full dbt duration; the lock is per-process so a worker-side materialization transform and an API-side trigger on the same tenant schema are not serialized, racing DROP/CREATE TABLE DDL. The entire dbt-execution path has zero non-mocked test coverage.

issues.title: Data Dictionary refresh destroys the data it just loaded
issues.summary: ACTIVELY DESTROYS PROD DATA ON EVERY CLICK. Cheapest correct fix: route the button to materialize_workspace (in-place reload + sibling rebuilds) and delete the _r-schema machinery. Put this FIRST in wave 1.

findings.title: Refresh loads fresh data into the old schema then destroys it (legacy /refresh/ path)
findings.chain: frontend/src/store/dictionarySlice.ts:197 POST /refresh/ -> apps/workspaces/api/views.py:362-365 create_refresh_schema ({base}_r{hex}, schema_manager.py:169-181) + defer -> apps/workspaces/tasks.py:150 create_physical_schema(new) -> tasks.py:173 run_pipeline(membership,...) [new schema never passed] -> mcp_server/services/materializer.py:183 SchemaManager().provision(tenant) -> schema_manager.py:66-78 resolves by base name, returns OLD ACTIVE schema; data loads there -> tasks.py:182-184 empty _r schema marked ACTIVE -> tasks.py:188-197 old schema TEARDOWN + teardown_schema deferred +30min -> tasks.py:609-663 DROP SCHEMA CASCADE, runs STALE, dependent view schemas FAILED
findings.claim: refresh_tenant_schema creates a new '_r'-suffixed TenantSchema but run_pipeline ignores it and calls SchemaManager().provision(tenant), which resolves by the unsuffixed sanitized external_id and returns the existing ACTIVE (old) schema; all data loads into the OLD active schema. The task then marks the empty '_r' schema ACTIVE and flips the data-bearing old schema to TEARDOWN with a 30-min delayed DROP SCHEMA CASCADE. Net: refresh destroys the data it just loaded, leaves an empty ACTIVE schema with zero MaterializationRun rows (empty catalog), routing is nondeterministic during the 30-min window (two ACTIVE rows, unordered .afirst()), dependent multi-tenant view schemas flip FAILED with no rebuild, and RefreshStatusView reports success. tests/test_refresh_task.py mocks run_pipeline in every test and even asserts the destructive teardown scheduling, so the suite cannot observe the bug and pins the broken contract.

findings.title: Refresh path never rebuilds dependent multi-tenant view schemas; teardown comment is false on that path
findings.claim: materialize_workspace defers sibling view-schema rebuilds (PR #230) but refresh_tenant_schema — which also replaces a shared tenant schema — does not; teardown_schema flips dependent view schemas to FAILED with a comment asserting 'the tenant's data is gone' which is false after a refresh (a new ACTIVE schema exists and a rebuild would succeed). Multi-tenant workspaces sharing a refreshed tenant stay FAILED until something else re-materializes.

issues.title: MCP teardown_schema updates Django state; add CAS at the drop site
issues.summary: The agent-exposed teardown tool drops physical schemas but never updates Django state and cascade-kills sibling workspaces; the queued teardown task drops resurrected rows with no state CAS. Fix or unbind the tool; add the CAS.

findings.title: MCP teardown_schema tool drops physical schemas but never updates any Django state, and destroys sibling workspaces' shared tenant schemas
findings.chain: agent tool call teardown_schema(confirm=True) (MCP_TOOL_NAMES, apps/agents/graph/base.py:65-76) -> mcp_server/server.py:801-865 mgr.ateardown_view_schema(vs)/mgr.ateardown(ts) -> apps/workspaces/services/schema_manager.py:474-512 DROP SCHEMA CASCADE only, no state writes -> contrast tasks.py:609-687 (EXPIRED, runs STALE, _fail_dependent_view_schemas) -> get_schema_status server.py:689-735 phantom tables; data-loss leg: materializer.py:564-605 reads PARTIAL cursor_state, writers skip DROP
findings.claim: The agent-exposed MCP teardown_schema tool calls SchemaManager.ateardown/ateardown_view_schema (physical DROP only; the docstring says 'callers are responsible for updating the model state') and updates nothing: TenantSchema rows stay ACTIVE over dropped schemas, MaterializationRuns stay COMPLETED (never flipped STALE), the workspace's WorkspaceViewSchema stays ACTIVE, and sibling multi-tenant workspaces whose views were cascade-dropped are never failed — unlike the worker teardown_schema task which does all of this (and got the PR #230 sibling-fail machinery the tool lacks). Consequences: get_schema_status reports exists=true/state=active with phantom tables from stale run.result while queries fail at SET ROLE (role dropped); tenant schemas are keyed by external_id and shared across workspaces, so the tool destroys other workspaces' queryable data despite its 'this workspace' framing; provision later recreates an empty physical schema under the still-ACTIVE row. Data-loss leg: a prior PARTIAL run's cursor_state survives (no STALE flip), so the next materialization resumes from the watermark into an empty recreated table, marking the source completed with pre-watermark rows silently missing. Only guards are confirm=True (LLM-suppliable) and workspace existence; no role or membership check.

findings.title: provision() resurrects TEARDOWN/EXPIRED rows but the already-queued teardown task drops them unconditionally (no state CAS)
findings.claim: A TenantSchema in TEARDOWN (TTL janitor flip, or refresh's 30-min delayed teardown) is resurrected to ACTIVE by provision()'s IntegrityError fall-through (and its TTL reset, per the 2026-06-10 incident-b fix), but the already-queued teardown_schema/teardown_view_schema_task fetch the row by id and DROP SCHEMA CASCADE unconditionally with no re-check that state is still TEARDOWN — so a materialization that races the janitor (or that runs during the refresh 30-min window or queue backlog after a worker outage) loads fresh data into a schema the stale teardown then drops, flipping runs STALE and the row EXPIRED. Surviving sibling of incident-b: TTL was reset on resurrect but teardown was not made state-aware. Same race for view schemas vs rebuild_workspace_view_schema.

issues.title: Recipe runner signature drift -- feature 100% dead since March
issues.summary: RecipeRunner calls build_agent_graph with a removed kwarg -> TypeError -> 500 -> RecipeRun stranded RUNNING forever. Triple drift (kwarg + initial-state + result-extraction). Restores the feature to working-as-designed; the redesign question is separate (see content-satellite-redesign).

findings.title: Recipe execution 100% broken: RecipeRunner calls build_agent_graph with removed tenant_membership signature
findings.chain: frontend/src/store/recipeSlice.ts:135 POST run -> apps/recipes/api/views.py:107-108 RecipeRunner.execute() -> apps/recipes/services/runner.py:189 RecipeRun created RUNNING -> runner.py:191 async_to_sync(_build_graph)() outside try -> runner.py:115-119 build_agent_graph(tenant_membership=...) -> apps/agents/graph/base.py:480-486 signature has no tenant_membership and requires workspace -> TypeError -> views.py:109-111 returns 500; RecipeRun stuck RUNNING; state drift runner.py:215-224 vs apps/agents/graph/state.py:80-148
findings.claim: RecipeRunner._build_graph calls build_agent_graph(tenant_membership=..., user=..., checkpointer=None) but the signature is build_agent_graph(workspace, user, checkpointer, mcp_tools, oauth_tokens) with no **kwargs and no tenant_membership param (drift introduced by e26cd75, 2026-03-10) — TypeError on every recipe run. The graph build happens outside the try block, so the API returns HTTP 500 and the RecipeRun row created beforehand is stranded RUNNING forever (no janitor reconciles RecipeRun). Triple drift even if the kwarg were fixed: initial_state uses pre-rename keys (tenant_id/tenant_name/tenant_membership_id, no workspace_id/thread_id) absent from AgentState, so MCP injection passes workspace_id='' and every data tool fails with VALIDATION_ERROR (3 in a row trips the escalation breaker); mcp_tools is never loaded/passed; and execute() calls sync graph.invoke() on an async-only graph (execute_async has zero production callers). All recipe tests @patch build_agent_graph with Mock/AsyncMock, masking the 3-month-old break; test_recipe_runner.py is four @skip placeholders.

issues.title: Artifact sandbox is a no-op (allow-scripts + allow-same-origin)
issues.summary: Drop allow-same-origin from the artifact iframe (runtime-verified to restore isolation). Closes prompt-injection -> session takeover: agent-generated code can currently issue credentialed state-changing requests as the viewer.

findings.title: Artifact sandbox iframe: allow-scripts + allow-same-origin neutralizes the sandbox; agent-generated code runs same-origin with full user API authority
findings.claim: The sandbox page is served same-origin and session-authenticated, executes agent-generated React via Babel.transform + new Function (and re-executes arbitrary <script> tags including remote src for html artifacts), and is embedded with sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-modals' — that combination on a same-origin document makes the sandbox a no-op. CSP allows 'unsafe-eval' and connect-src 'self', and CSRF_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=False makes the CSRF token JS-readable, so artifact code can issue credentialed state-changing /api/ requests as the viewing user and reach window.parent. Artifacts are agent-generated code steerable by prompt injection from materialized third-party data (e.g. OCS message content) or by any read_write member: stored XSS / exfiltration across workspace members with full API reach. ArtifactPanel's postMessage listener also lacks an origin check. Server-side PNG/PDF export (if wired) loads agent HTML into headless Chromium with no CSP/egress restriction (SSRF). The docs' 'sandboxed rendering' claim does not match the implementation.

issues.title: Multi-tenant live artifacts query the wrong schema / show zero artifacts
issues.summary: Route artifact query-data through load_workspace_context (view schema, not first tenant's schema); populate Artifact.conversation_id so shared/public threads stop showing zero artifacts and the dead render_url is fixed.

findings.title: Live-query artifacts in multi-tenant workspaces execute against the first tenant's schema, not the view schema
findings.chain: sandbox JS fetch /api/workspaces/<id>/artifacts/<id>/query-data/ (apps/artifacts/views.py:254) -> ArtifactQueryDataView.get views.py:795 tenants.afirst() -> views.py:800 load_tenant_context(tenant.external_id) -> execute_query search_path=t_<first> while agent SQL targets ws_* views (mcp_server/context.py:83-139); correct router load_workspace_context routes multi-tenant to the view schema
findings.claim: ArtifactQueryDataView resolves the query context via workspace.tenants.afirst() (alphabetical by canonical_name) + load_tenant_context(external_id) instead of load_workspace_context, while the agent authored source_queries against the ws_* view schema's namespaced {prefix}__{table} views (the only surface it sees via load_workspace_context). At render time those queries execute with search_path on one tenant's t_* schema, so every live artifact in a multi-tenant workspace errors with 'relation does not exist' (or, for bare raw_* names, silently shows one tenant's slice as the workspace union — and adding a tenant silently changes which subset is shown). Every other consumer was migrated to load_workspace_context; this one wasn't. The TTL touch also targets the wrong (first tenant) schema. Affects the sandbox auto-fetch, Data tab, export, and shared/public artifact pages.

findings.title: Artifact.conversation_id never populated; shared/public threads always show zero artifacts; tool returns dead render_url
findings.chain: apps/agents/graph/base.py:694 create_artifact_tools(workspace, user) [no conversation_id] -> apps/agents/tools/artifact_tool.py:57,197,209 conversation_id='' + render_url f-string -> apps/chat/thread_views.py:52-68 filter(conversation_id=str(thread_id)) returns [] for public_thread_view; render_url vs apps/artifacts/urls.py:29-33
findings.claim: _build_tools calls create_artifact_tools(workspace, user) without conversation_id, so every chat-created artifact stores conversation_id=''. The only consumers (_get_thread_artifacts / public_thread_view for shared threads) filter conversation_id=str(thread_id) and always return [], so shared/public thread pages silently omit all artifacts and the indexed conversation_id field is dead. The tool also returns render_url='/artifacts/<id>/render/' which matches no route (real route is /api/workspaces/<wsid>/artifacts/<id>/sandbox/), so if the agent surfaces it as a link it 404s. The public page also only dumps artifact code as <pre> (no sandbox iframe, no live data fetch), so a 'shared dashboard' is a code listing.

issues.title: dbt runs arbitrary user SQL as managed-DB superuser; generated models fail
issues.summary: Confine dbt: dedicated low-privilege role + search_path (or gate transform writes behind validation). Same change fixes the generated CommCare staging models that fail silently, and the workspace-scope transforms that never run.

findings.title: Transform assets execute arbitrary user SQL via dbt as the managed-DB superuser — no validation, no SET ROLE downgrade, no schema confinement
findings.chain: POST /api/transformations/assets/ (views.py:54 IsAuthenticated, own-tenant; models.py:100 validates only name) -> next materialization tenant stage (executor.py:50-73) -> generate_profiles_yml uses MANAGED_DATABASE_URL superuser, no search_path/SET ROLE (executor.py:134, dbt_runner.py:28) -> dbt executes fully-qualified cross-tenant SQL -> materializes victim data into attacker schema -> read via normal readonly path
findings.claim: TransformationAsset.sql_content is free-text (only name is regex-validated) and writable via the serializer; the executor runs it through dbt connecting as the full MANAGED_DATABASE_URL role (the superuser/owner that runs CREATE SCHEMA/CREATE ROLE) with no SET ROLE downgrade, no search_path lockdown, and no SQL validation — unlike the query MCP tool (SET ROLE _ro + validator). A tenant member can POST a scope=TENANT asset whose body reads another tenant's schema (SELECT * FROM t_victim.raw_cases) or does DDL/COPY/create roles via jinja run_query, which dbt materializes into the attacker's own schema for normal readonly retrieval; it runs automatically on the next materialization's tenant stage or via POST /api/transformations/runs/trigger/. Complete bypass of the read-only/tenant-isolation guarantees the query path enforces.

findings.title: dbt staging models can't resolve raw tables: generated SQL is unqualified and the dbt profile sets no search_path — every generated CommCare staging model fails silently
findings.chain: materializer.py:217 commcare assets generated -> :452 _run_transform_phase -> executor.py:134 db_url=MANAGED_DATABASE_URL -> dbt_runner.py:28-61 no search_path -> commcare_staging.py:144 'FROM raw_cases' unqualified -> dbt CREATE TABLE t_<id>.stg_x AS (SELECT ... FROM raw_cases) resolved against $user,public -> relation does not exist; swallowed: executor.py:85-92 + materializer.py:455-477 COMPLETED + tasks.py:961-982 ignores transforms
findings.claim: The CommCare staging generator emits model SQL with unqualified FROM raw_cases/FROM raw_forms, but generate_profiles_yml sets no search_path and dbt-postgres only applies one from the profile, so dbt creates the model relation in the tenant schema while executing the SELECT body against the default search_path ('$user', public) where the raw tables do not exist — every generated staging model fails at dbt runtime with 'relation does not exist'. The breakage is triply silent: transform failures are isolated (no re-raise), the materializer still marks the run COMPLETED, the resume aggregator reads only result['sources'] (never result['transforms']), the agent is told 'completed', the ThreadJob goes green, and all dbt tests are mocked. Same swallowed-failure class as the view-schema variant fixed in PR #229, but the transform variant was not. System-asset generation failures are also swallowed ('continuing pipeline') and compose with this.

findings.title: Workspace-scope transformations never run during materialization; stale dbt tables presented as fresh; orphaned system assets keep executing
findings.claim: _run_transform_phase calls run_transformation_pipeline without a workspace argument and the executor only appends the workspace stage when workspace is truthy, so scope=WORKSPACE TransformationAssets are executed only by the manual /runs/trigger/ endpoint (no frontend UI) — creatable via API but never materialized in the shipped product. Because dbt assets are materialized as tables (+materialized:table), a workspace-scoped model's table survives raw-table reloads with pre-refresh contents and (multi-tenant) gets re-published via the view schema next to fresh data with no staleness marker. Separately, upsert_system_assets only update_or_creates and never deletes assets the latest metadata no longer produces, so a renamed/removed CommCare form/case-type leaves an orphan stg_* asset that keeps being selected, handed to dbt, and listed to the agent (stale table or silent failure).

issues.title: Onboarding 'Use an API Key' POSTs to a deleted endpoint (guaranteed 404)
issues.summary: First-run path for every non-OAuth user 404s. Re-point to the live endpoint.

findings.title: Onboarding 'Use an API Key' form POSTs to the deleted /api/auth/tenant-credentials/ endpoint — guaranteed 404 on the critical first-run path
findings.chain: App.tsx:65 renders OnboardingWizard when !onboarding_complete (auth_views.py:72-90) -> OnboardingWizard.tsx:27 api.post('/api/auth/tenant-credentials/', {provider,tenant_id,tenant_name,credential}) -> apps/users/auth_urls.py (no such path; only connections/) -> 404 -> OnboardingWizard.tsx:36-37 'Failed to save credentials'
findings.claim: Every new user without OAuth is forced into OnboardingWizard (onboarding_complete is false until a connection-backed TenantMembership exists), whose 'Use an API Key' form POSTs to /api/auth/tenant-credentials/ with the pre-#220 payload shape. That route was removed in the TenantConnection rebuild (ad56a65, replaced by /api/auth/connections/ with {provider, fields}), so the wizard guarantees a 404 -> 'Failed to save credentials' dead end. The wizard is a pre-rebuild fossil that also only offers CommCare while ApiConnectionDialog supports the full provider registry; the bug survives because the frontend API types are hand-written.

issues.title: Three one-liners with outsized blast radius
issues.summary: Fail-CLOSED thread-ownership except (currently fails open -> foreign thread append); resume-prompt else-branch honesty (stops telling the agent a FAILED run 'just completed'); reconciler staleness measured against the resume job (stops falsely failing healthy long materializations).

findings.title: Chat thread-ownership check fails open on broad except
findings.claim: chat_view wraps the foreign-thread lookup in 'except Exception: existing_thread = None' (intended only for non-UUID thread ids), so any transient ORM error skips the ownership rejection and the turn proceeds; the LangGraph checkpointer writes messages keyed solely by thread_id with no ownership concept, so a request carrying another user's thread UUID during a DB blip can append turns into that user's conversation. A security check converted to fail-open by an over-broad catch; fix is catching only (ValueError, ValidationError).

findings.title: Resume prompt else-branch tells the agent the materialization 'just completed ... using the now-loaded data' for fully FAILED and CANCELLED runs
findings.chain: tasks.py:1052 _aggregate_materialization_state returns status='failed' when any run FAILED -> tasks.py:1075-1119 if/elif covers view_schema_failed/no_runs/partial only -> tasks.py:1120-1125 else interpolates status into 'just completed ... using the now-loaded data' -> agent.ainvoke with that framing
findings.claim: resume_thread_after_materialization branches on view_schema_failed / no_runs / partial explicitly (tasks.py:1075-1119), but status='failed' (all sources failed — exactly what token expiry before first commit or auth revocation produces) and 'cancelled' fall to the generic else branch (tasks.py:1120-1125): 'Materialization just completed (status=failed). Please continue with the user's original request using the now-loaded data.' For a total failure there is no loaded data; the instruction contradicts the embedded status and invites the agent to query empty/absent schemas — the documented precondition for the #190 panic-loop class. The 'partial' branch shows the intended honest pattern; 'failed' never got one. The resume task always fires via the finally block (tasks.py:356-360).

findings.title: Stale-job reconciler falsely fails healthy in-flight resumes for materializations whose dispatch-to-resume exceeds 10 minutes
findings.chain: frontend/src/hooks/useWorkspaceJobs.ts:4 3s poll -> apps/workspaces/api/jobs_views.py:123-135 reconcile any active tj older than 10min -> apps/workspaces/tasks.py:742-748 materialize job status 'succeeded' not in {todo,doing} -> tasks.py:750-774 RUNNING CAS->FAILED + synthetic message racing tasks.py:1161 ainvoke -> tasks.py:1264-1272 resume terminal CAS matches 0 rows
findings.claim: reconcile_stale_thread_job measures staleness from ThreadJob.created_at (includes full materialization + queue time) and checks the procrastinate status of the MATERIALIZE job, not the resume job (whose id is never recorded). For any materialization taking >10 min, once the resume task claims PENDING->RUNNING and enters agent.ainvoke (up to 120s), the materialize job's status is 'succeeded', so the RUNNING branch CAS-flips the ThreadJob to FAILED and injects a synthetic 'interrupted (server restart)' AIMessage into the same LangGraph thread the resume is concurrently writing. The frontend's 3s active-jobs poll runs the API-side backstop (added e91ff9b) making this near-deterministic with a tab open; the */15 worker janitor hits it probabilistically. The real answer still arrives and interleaves; the resume's terminal CAS then matches 0 rows. ThreadJob has no started_at/resume-job reference to distinguish crashed from in-flight.

issues.title: Cross-tenant metadata disclosure via unqualified pg_catalog reads
issues.summary: pg_catalog is world-readable regardless of SET ROLE and is advertised in the system prompt; tenant schema names are customer identifiers and reltuples leaks row counts. (SET ROLE does block actual cross-tenant DATA reads.)

findings.title: Cross-tenant metadata disclosure via unqualified pg_catalog reads, advertised in the system prompt; SQL validator gaps where SET ROLE is the only defense
findings.claim: SQLValidator only checks schema-qualified table references; unqualified pg_namespace/pg_class/pg_views/pg_tables resolve via the implicit pg_catalog search_path entry and are world-readable in PG16 regardless of SET ROLE, and tenant schema names are derived from tenant.external_id (customer/domain identifiers) with pg_class.reltuples leaking other tenants' approximate row counts — the system prompt explicitly tells the agent these catalog views are reachable, so any chat user or prompt-injected content can enumerate the shared DB's customer list via the query tool. _validate_statement_type also checks only the top-level expression type, so SELECT ... INTO and data-modifying CTEs (WITH x AS (INSERT...) SELECT) pass, and pg_sleep/set_config are absent from DANGEROUS_FUNCTIONS — defense-in-depth erosion currently backstopped only by the _ro role grants/PG16 defaults. The view-schema _ro role also retains SELECT/USAGE on removed tenants' schemas after a rebuild (no revoke), a latent cross-tenant read primitive fenced only by the validator's schema allowlist.

issues.title: OCS participants sync is team-wide, not chatbot-scoped
issues.summary: The 'chatbot' param Scout sends is documented upstream but unimplemented, so whole-team rosters land in a single-chatbot tenant schema. Plus: team-mismatch currently surfaces as a generic 'No credential configured'.

findings.title: OCS participants sync is team-wide, not chatbot-scoped: Scout sends a 'chatbot' param upstream silently ignores, loading whole-team rosters + per-chatbot custom data into a single-chatbot tenant schema
findings.chain: pipelines/ocs_sync.yml source 'participants' -> ocs_participants.py:44-47 params={'chatbot': experiment_id} -> upstream participants.py get() reads only 'experiment' -> unfiltered qs=Participant.objects.filter(team=request.team) with all-chatbot data_qs prefetch -> materializer ~L1379 ON CONFLICT (participant_id) writes whole team into raw_participants
findings.claim: Scout's OCSParticipantLoader passes params={'chatbot': experiment_id}, matching the OCS OpenAPI docs. But the upstream view's get() body (verified verbatim from dimagi/open-chat-studio@main apps/api/views/participants.py) reads only 'identifier', 'platform', and 'experiment' from query_params — the documented 'chatbot' param is unimplemented and DRF ignores unknown params. The filter therefore never applies: every OCS participants materialization loads ALL participants of the whole OCS team, each with their per-chatbot custom 'data' arrays for EVERY chatbot in the team (unfiltered data_qs prefetched and serialized), and writes them into a tenant schema whose boundary is one chatbot. Cross-chatbot participant rosters and custom data (PII-class payloads) become queryable by the agent and workspace members granted only this chatbot. Invisible on single-chatbot teams. Scout-side fix is one token: send 'experiment' instead of 'chatbot'.

findings.title: Multi-team OCS team-mismatch surfaces as generic 'No credential configured', indistinguishable from no connection
findings.claim: A user has at most one OCS OAuth TenantConnection; each membership records its team and aresolve_credential fails closed when the live token's team != the membership's team (correct, prevents cross-team leaks). But the failure is reported to the agent/user as the generic 'No credential configured' — identical to having no connection at all — so a user with a valid OCS login in team B materializing a chatbot scoped to team A is never told to re-authorize into the other team. Same indistinguishability applies to Fernet-decrypt failure, refresh failure (which then 401s), and team mismatch — four distinct failures collapse into one misleading message; the AUTH_TOKEN_EXPIRED envelope code exists but is unused on this path.

issues.title: Live/reload tool-output rich cards broken (toolCallId, 0-tables, truncation)
issues.summary: toolCallId mismatch kills per-card progress/Stop live; get_metadata renders '0 tables' on reload (Array.isArray over a map); 2000-char live truncation breaks success cards live; thinking blocks dropped on reload; error-envelope info discarded; the apostrophe->double-quote parse hack.

findings.title: Live-stream toolCallId mismatch kills per-card materialization progress/Stop/failure affordances during live sessions
findings.chain: apps/chat/stream.py:190 toolCallId=run_id vs apps/agents/graph/base.py:462-469 + mcp_server/server.py:623-628 tool_call_id=toolu_... -> jobs_views.py:52 returns toolu_... -> ChatMessage.tsx:161-163 equality fails -> matchingJob/matchingFailure null live
findings.claim: The SSE stream emits toolCallId = LangGraph run UUID while ThreadJob.tool_call_id is the LLM tool-call id (toolu_...) injected by the graph and persisted by the MCP server. The frontend scopes the in-card materialization progress block, Stop button, and failure card by strict equality on these ids, so they never render during a live streaming session — only after the transcript is reloaded from the checkpointer (whose converter emits the real LLM id). The thread-level banner (matched by thread_id) masks the worst of it; the fix is one line since the stream already holds the ToolMessage with .tool_call_id.

findings.title: get_metadata rich card renders '0 tables' on every successful reload: Array.isArray over an object map
findings.chain: metadata.py:342-346 tables as dict -> server.py:276-285 success_response dict tables + table_count -> message_converter.py:69 full JSON -> ChatMessage.tsx:66-67 dispatch get_metadata card -> ToolOutput.tsx:313 Array.isArray(dict)=false -> :318 renders '0 tables'
findings.claim: Backend get_metadata sends data.tables as a dict keyed by table name (plus a correct scalar table_count), but GetMetadataOutput counts tables via Array.isArray(output.data.tables) ? length : 0, which is always 0 for an object. Whenever the rich card is reachable (thread reload / shared page — live is always truncation-broken), it shows 'Metadata loaded · 0 tables' with a green check for a snapshot that contains tables. The TS type data:{tables: unknown[]} codifies the wrong shape and omits schema/table_count/relationships.

findings.title: Per-tool live/reload split refines the truncation finding: query/describe/list rich cards fail live for typical payloads but work on reload; self-inflicted indent-2 whitespace bloat; live tool cards appear only at tool-end with isLoading states unreachable
findings.chain: func_metadata.py:531 indent=2 JSON -> stream.py:47-73 re-pretty-print indent=2 -> stream.py:200-210 slice 2000 -> ChatMessage.tsx:24-53 JSON.parse fails -> :300-303 <pre> fallback; reload: message_converter.py:69 full string -> parse succeeds -> rich card; stream.py:159-210 single emission at on_tool_end -> ChatMessage.tsx:153 isLoading never true live
findings.claim: Measured against the exact indent-2 serialization: the 2000-char live truncation (stream.py:200-203) makes the truncated pretty JSON unparseable, so rich cards degrade to raw mangled JSON live at ~7+ tables (list_tables), ~11+ columns (describe_table), ~>10 short rows (query); get_metadata always exceeds 2000. The identical call renders a correct rich card after thread reload (converter sends the full string). Error envelopes are small, so error cards ironically work live while success cards don't. Payload bloat is self-inflicted: FastMCP pretty-prints at indent 2 and stream.py re-pretty-prints, spending most of the 2000 budget on whitespace. Distinct timing half: stream.py has no on_tool_start handler — tool-input-available and tool-output-available are emitted back-to-back at on_tool_end, so during a long query the live UI shows no tool card at all and ToolCallPart's input-streaming/input-available isLoading states are unreachable.

findings.title: Tool-output rich rendering depends on a global apostrophe->double-quote replace; live stream sends empty tool input and 2000-char-truncated unparseable output
findings.claim: ChatMessage.parseOutput handles Python-repr-shaped MCP envelopes by output.replace(/'/g,'"') before JSON.parse, so any apostrophe in data (e.g. "St. John's") breaks the parse and the rich tool card silently degrades to a raw <pre> dump (adversarial quote mixes could parse into wrong JSON). This is a frontend patch over a backend serialization leak (tool content arriving as Python repr instead of JSON). The live SSE stream compounds it: it emits tool-input-available with input:{} (the SQL/args never shown live, undercutting the prompt's mandatory provenance contract), only handles on_tool_end (no loading states), and truncates output at 2000 chars with a suffix that makes it unparseable JSON — so the rich QueryToolOutput table fails to render live for any non-trivial result; the checkpointer reload path supplies full args/untruncated output, so the same message renders differently after refresh.

findings.title: Thread reload silently drops reasoning/thinking parts: message_converter emits only text and tool parts
findings.chain: stream.py:138-146 reasoning chunks live -> message_converter.py:39-53 only text blocks extracted, no reasoning part -> ChatMessage.tsx:385-387 reasoning renderer never receives a part on reload; auto-reload ChatPanel.tsx:126-131
findings.claim: Live stream emits reasoning-start/delta/end and ChatMessage renders a Thinking block, but message_converter filters AIMessage content to type=='text' blocks and never emits a reasoning part, so all Thinking blocks vanish on any reload — including the automatic post-materialization messageReloadKey refetch that replaces the in-memory messages mid-session.

findings.title: Tool error-envelope information discarded by rich cards and get_schema_status envelope self-inconsistency: error fields omitted from TS types, timing_ms missing, FAILED variant nests error string inside data
findings.chain: server.py:217-221 NOT_FOUND with message -> envelope.py:57-67 -> ToolOutput.tsx:170-184/247-260/303-308 no error field -> :188/264/312 generic text; server.py:667-798 get_schema_status branches omit timing_ms and FAILED returns success:true with data.error string vs envelope.py:34-67 contract
findings.claim: describe_table/list_tables/get_metadata error envelopes carry {code, message, detail?} but DescribeTableOutput/ListTablesOutput/GetMetadataOutput interfaces have no error field and components render only generic 'Failed to ...' strings, discarding e.g. "Table 'x' not found in schema 'y'"; QueryOutput.error is stricter than runtime (omits detail). Separately, get_schema_status is envelope-inconsistent: no branch passes timing_ms, and the FAILED-view-schema branch returns success:true with data.error as a string — the only place an error lives inside data rather than top-level {error:{code,message}}, so generic success==false / top-level-error checks classify a failed view-schema build as success (the same drift class behind the 'agent told completed' incident).

findings.title: Reload fallback display silently slices output to 2000 chars with no marker despite having the full string client-side
findings.chain: message_converter.py:69 full string -> ChatMessage.tsx:198-200 fallbackText -> :300-303 slice(0,2000) no marker
findings.claim: ChatMessage.tsx fallback renders fallbackText.slice(0, 2000) — affecting the six card-less tools (get_lineage, list_pipelines, get/cancel_materialization, get_schema_status, teardown_schema) and any unparseable rich-tool payload — with no truncation indicator, unlike the live path which appends '... (truncated, N chars total)'. The cap is display-only; the data is already in memory.

findings.title: TS-vs-runtime contract inventory: note string|null, JSONB cells render '[object Object]', warnings/project_id untyped, apostrophe-replace branch vestigial, output-error state unproducible
findings.chain: func_metadata.py:531 double-quoted JSON never repr -> ChatMessage.tsx:26-32 replace branch can't return for non-array -> :34-35 real parse; ToolOutput.tsx:139 String(cell); ChatMessage.tsx:154 dead output-error branch
findings.claim: ListTablesOutput.note typed string? vs runtime string|null; entries omit materialized_at and columns omit default (looser, benign); QueryOutput rows cells can be JSONB objects rendered as '[object Object]' via String(cell); envelope project_id/warnings untyped except query; six of ten tools have no TS contract; parseOutput's apostrophe-replace branch (ChatMessage.tsx:26-32) is vestigial under mcp 1.26/adapter 0.2.1 — FastMCP emits double-quoted JSON, the branch parses a non-array and falls through, so rendering actually hinges on the second JSON.parse, correcting the known finding's mechanism; part.state 'output-error' is producible by neither stream nor converter so ToolCallPart's check is dead code.

issues.title: Pages don't refetch / clear state on workspace switch
issues.summary: Artifacts/Recipes show stale cross-workspace data then 404 (threadId fix reset only threadId); ConnectionsPage guard compares the wrong ids (never fires); WorkspaceDetailPage never clears a prior load error.

findings.title: Artifacts and Recipes pages don't refetch on workspace switch: stale cross-workspace data then 404 actions
findings.chain: TopBar workspace switcher -> WorkspaceSwitcher select() setActiveDomain -> RecipesPage.tsx:45-47 useEffect deps [fetchRecipes] only -> list still workspace A's -> recipeSlice fetchRecipe reads new activeDomainId -> GET /api/workspaces/<B>/recipes/<recipe-of-A>/ -> apps/recipes/api/views.py:55-57 404
findings.claim: RecipesPage and ArtifactsPage fetch only on mount (effect deps exclude activeDomainId; zustand actions are stable refs), while KnowledgePage/DataDictionaryPage correctly refetch on workspace change. Switching workspaces via the always-present TopBar switcher while on /recipes or /artifacts leaves the previous workspace's items on screen; subsequent actions (fetchRecipe, runRecipe, openArtifact->sandbox) combine the NEW activeDomainId with OLD object ids and 404. The threadId-leak incident fix (00c423d) reset only threadId in setActiveDomain; no general per-workspace state reset exists. Same problem solved two different ways across four sibling pages.

findings.title: ConnectionsPage post-removal workspace-switch guard compares workspace ids to TenantMembership ids — can never fire
findings.claim: After removing a connection, confirmRemove builds removedMembershipIds from chatbot membership_id values (str(TenantMembership.id)) and tests removedMembershipIds.has(activeDomainId) where activeDomainId is a Workspace id from workspaceApi.list(). The disjoint id spaces make the guard always false, so the 'switch away from a workspace whose data source was just removed' recovery never runs (and storeDomains in the closure is a stale pre-refetch snapshot). Residue of the old domains=tenant-memberships model.

findings.title: WorkspaceDetailPage never clears a prior load error: subsequent successful loads still render the error screen
findings.claim: The workspace fetch effect sets loading but never resets error; the render gate is `if (error || !workspace)`. After one failed load (stale/dead workspace link), navigating in-place to a valid workspace (same route element, e.g. via the topbar gear) succeeds but the page keeps showing the error until a full remount.

issues.title: BASE_PATH-bypassing URLs break the labs /scout deployment + widget SDK
issues.summary: Root-relative URLs break health poll, sandbox iframe, public share pages on labs; widget setMode/theme are no-ops and widget.js isn't routed; DEPLOY_ENVIRONMENT mislabels labs as development. (Labs infra is out-of-repo.)

findings.title: BASE_PATH-bypassing root-relative URLs break labs /scout deployment: health poll, artifact sandbox iframe, and public share pages
findings.claim: Several frontend/backend URLs are hardcoded root-relative without the BASE_PATH prefix, breaking the labs deployment built with VITE_BASE_PATH=/scout/ (nginx only proxies /scout/...). (1) NetworkStatusContext fetches '/health/' raw, so the 5s health poll monitors whatever the labs host serves at root /health/ — the network indicator is permanently green-while-down or red-with-suppression of all page error states (dishonest-indicator class). (2) ArtifactPanel's sandbox iframe src and the Django-served sandbox HTML's /api/ fetch are root-relative, so the artifact View tab 404s in the main app and embed widget under /scout. (3) PublicThreadPage/PublicRecipeRunPage re-derive the share token from the unstripped pathname with a regex anchored at ^/shared/, so under /scout the token is undefined, the fetch returns early, and every public share link renders an endless skeleton. Root-mounted Kamal prod is unaffected. nginx.prod-kamal.conf also lacks a /widget.js location so the embed SDK URL 404s on scout.dimagi.com.

findings.title: Widget SDK setMode()/theme are no-ops; host-specific '/labs/scout/' hardcode in embed OAuth fallback
findings.claim: widget.js exposes setMode (posts scout:set-mode) and forwards a theme URL param, but EmbedPage's scout:set-mode handler only console.logs and embed mode is read once from a memoized URL hook so runtime setMode can't change it; theme is parsed with no consumer (only setTenant works). Separately, in embed mode with an empty document.referrer the OAuth next URL falls back to the literal ConnectLabs-specific '/labs/scout/', sending users on any other embedding host to a 404 after OAuth. Widget version is a hand-bumped tag.

findings.title: CloudFormation stack describes production only; labs runs on out-of-repo ECS Fargate infra (deploy-target drift)
findings.chain: infra/scout-stack.yml (production-only) vs .github/workflows/deploy-labs.yml:21-30,239 (ECS Fargate, account 858923557655, assignPublicIp=ENABLED, LABS_SECURITY_GROUP/LABS_SUBNET) and frontend/nginx.prod.conf (references /ecs/labs-jj-scout-web)
findings.claim: infra/scout-stack.yml provisions only the production stack (Kamal on single EC2 + RDS + ElastiCache + ECR for scout.dimagi.com). The labs deployment is a different runtime governed by infra not in this repo: ECS Fargate in account 858923557655, cluster labs-jj-cluster, services labs-jj-scout-{web,mcp,worker}, with assignPublicIp=ENABLED and opaque LABS_SECURITY_GROUP/LABS_SUBNET. Findings derived from the stack ('8100 unreachable', '30-day retention', 'Redis unused', 'no alarms', 'no egress') are production-stack facts that may differ on labs; assignPublicIp=ENABLED gives labs task ENIs public IPs, and labs reachability depends entirely on the unauditable LABS_SECURITY_GROUP.

findings.title: DEPLOY_ENVIRONMENT heuristic mislabels connectlabs as 'development' for Sentry/Task Badger; three hand-maintained env blocks drift (MCP lacks Task Badger/Langfuse keys)
findings.claim: base.py sets DEPLOY_ENVIRONMENT='production' only when the settings module ends with '.production', but connectlabs.py inherits production posture with a module name ending '.connectlabs', so Sentry/Task Badger default to environment='development' for labs unless ECS task defs override (unverifiable). The three backend Kamal env blocks (api/worker/mcp) each hand-list vars and drift: deploy-mcp.yml omits TASKBADGER_API_KEY and Langfuse keys, yet mcp_server defers materialize_workspace — so agent-initiated materializations (the primary path) are invisible to Task Badger while API/worker-deferred tasks are tracked. No shared env manifest; each gap is discovered by outage. Server entrypoints (asgi/wsgi/manage) also setdefault development settings (DEBUG=True), so a prod container losing the settings env var silently boots permissive instead of crashing (only the MCP entrypoint fails fast).

issues.title: Finish the single-tenant -> multi-tenant retrofit (first-tenant shim)
issues.summary: DESIGN-GATED (what SHOULD a multi-tenant workspace show?). The first-tenant compat shim silently drives Data Dictionary, refresh, knowledge, recipe TTL; never-materialized multi-tenant workspaces spin forever; zero-tenant workspaces dead-end in chat.

findings.title: Single-tenant 'first tenant' compat shim silently drives whole features for multi-tenant workspaces (Data Dictionary, refresh, knowledge, recipe TTL)
findings.claim: Workspace.tenant/.external_tenant_id/.tenant_name are compatibility shims returning tenants.first() (alphabetical). DataDictionaryView, TableDetailView, RefreshSchemaView, and RefreshStatusView operate on that single tenant for both the 503 gate and the table list, so multi-tenant workspaces show only the alphabetically-first tenant's t_* tables (under raw names, never the prefixed ws_* views the agent queries) — annotations written there can never apply, the second tenant is invisible, and if tenant 1's schema expired while the view schema is ACTIVE the dictionary 503s while chat works. The refresh button refreshes only one tenant; RecipeRunView TTL-touches only the first tenant's schema, so a recipes-only multi-tenant workspace lets its other constituent schemas TTL-expire, after which teardown flips the view schema FAILED and the workspace goes dark despite active use.

findings.title: Never-materialized multi-tenant workspace reports 'provisioning' forever: perpetual fake 'Loading data...' spinner
findings.claim: _derive_schema_status returns 'provisioning' for any multi-tenant workspace whose WorkspaceViewSchema state is neither ACTIVE nor FAILED — including the no-row case (view_states.get(w.id) is None for a workspace never materialized). The frontend maps provisioning -> 'loading' and renders an animated spinner titled 'Loading data...' in the switcher and workspaces list, so a 2-tenant workspace created and never chatted in shows an infinite spinner implying active work, violating the honest-progress norm.

findings.title: Chat dead-end in tenant-less workspaces: UI allows zero-tenant workspaces, backend 403, frontend generic error
findings.claim: The UI allows creating a workspace with zero data sources; for such workspaces chat_view returns 403 'No tenant membership' (helpers returns tm=None, is_multi=False when tenants.afirst() is None) and the frontend shows the full chat UI plus a generic 'Something went wrong' with no guidance to add a data source. WorkspaceListView.post permits zero-tenant workspaces with no prevention or cleanup; downstream surfaces degrade to 503/403.

issues.title: One permission layer enforced on the content surface
issues.summary: DESIGN-GATED (what should READ/RW/MANAGE actually permit? should recipe is_shared/is_public exist at all?). Today DRF permission classes have zero importers; READ members mutate artifacts/knowledge/recipes and drive destructive agent tools. Honor archived_at uniformly; fix dead role tests.

findings.title: WorkspaceRole unenforced: DRF permission classes are dead code; READ members can mutate knowledge/recipes/artifacts and drive destructive agent tools via chat
findings.claim: IsWorkspaceMember/IsWorkspaceReadWrite/IsWorkspaceManager in apps/workspaces/permissions.py have zero imports anywhere; all role enforcement is inline and exists only on workspace management (MANAGE), /refresh/ (RW+), table-annotation PUT, and transformations writes. Everything else — artifacts PATCH/DELETE/undelete/export, knowledge CRUD/import, recipes CRUD/run/share-toggle, jobs cancel, materialization cancel/retry — gates on membership only, so a READ-role member can rename/delete artifacts, delete/import knowledge (KnowledgeEntry is injected verbatim into every member's system prompt -> stored prompt injection), and via POST /api/chat/ (where user_role is hardcoded 'analyst') drive the agent to create_artifact/save_learning/save_recipe plus MCP run_materialization and teardown_schema (destructive). AgentState.user_role docstring claims viewer/analyst/admin enforcement that no code implements.

findings.title: Recipe is_shared / is_public privacy toggles enforce nothing; 'private' recipes/runs visible and runnable by all members; share-creation UI gone but public endpoints live
findings.claim: save_as_recipe tool, model help_text, and the UI 'Share with project' checkbox all promise is_shared=False means only the creator can see/run a recipe, but every recipe/run view filters by workspace only — never by is_shared or created_by — so all recipes and runs are visible, editable, runnable, and deletable by every member; the agent actively makes a false privacy claim. RecipeRun.is_public is settable via PATCH but has no UI; Recipe.is_public/share_token are dead model fields whose save() still mints secrets.token_urlsafe(32). Thread/recipe-run public endpoints, token minting, and public pages remain live after the 2026-06-04 share-UI removal (9783eb2 was UI-only), so pre-removal tokens still grant unauthenticated access to messages + full artifact/recipe code+data with no revocation or audit UI; PublicRecipePage is unrouted and fetches a nonexistent endpoint; uiSlice.updateThreadSharing/setActiveDomainByTenantId are dead with phantom is_public/share_token types the backend never returns.

findings.title: Disconnect/disappear paths handle archived_at inconsistently across 5+ authz sites; tenant resolution is additive-only so upstream-revoked access stays readable
findings.claim: archived_at on TenantMembership is honored by tenant listing, connections views, and materialize_workspace (filter archived_at__isnull=True) but ignored by chat's single-tenant guard, member-add shared-tenant check, workspace-create/tenant-add validation, MCP run_materialization guard, and /refresh/ lookup — so disconnecting a credential revokes connections-UI visibility but not workspace/chat read access (reads need no credential; aresolve_credential returns None so only re-materialization fails). Tenant resolution only upserts/clears archived_at for tenants the provider currently returns and never archives memberships for tenants that disappeared upstream (no reaper), so a user removed from a domain/opportunity/team upstream keeps the membership, still appears in tenant lists, and retains read access to already-materialized data. disconnect_provider_view also deletes TenantConnection by URL provider class id while SocialAccount.provider may be a custom provider_id, silently no-op'ing archival. Removing a tenant from a workspace also never prunes WorkspaceMembership, orphaning members who shared only that tenant.

findings.title: Tenant-membership requirement asymmetric: multi-tenant chat waives the TenantMembership check single-tenant chat enforces
findings.claim: _resolve_workspace_and_membership requires a provider-verified TenantMembership for single-tenant chat but returns success with tm=None for any multi-tenant workspace, requiring only WorkspaceMembership; chat_view skips the per-tenant guard when is_multi_tenant. Member-add only requires sharing >=1 of the workspace's tenants, so a user with TenantMembership for T1 alone gets full SQL read access to T2/T3's data (view-schema queries run as the platform readonly role, not user credentials) in an A+B+C workspace, while a 1-tenant invite does not. The single-tenant TenantMembership check becomes security theater; whether workspace-membership-as-grant or provider-verification is intended is documented nowhere.

findings.title: test_workspace_permissions.py has zero role assertions and dead role fixtures; permissions.py has zero importers anywhere
findings.chain: grep finds zero importers of permissions.py classes; test_workspace_permissions.py:18-27 read/write fixtures unused, tests assert member-vs-non-member only; role enforcement tested only on transformations+refresh
findings.claim: Adjudication of the dead-code question, strengthened: IsWorkspaceMember/IsWorkspaceReadWrite/IsWorkspaceManager in apps/workspaces/permissions.py are not 'imported but ineffective' — grep across apps/ and tests/ finds zero importers; they are entirely unreferenced. The suite named test_workspace_permissions.py defines read_client/write_client fixtures (lines 18-27) used by no test, and every test asserts member-vs-non-member only. Role enforcement exists and is tested solely on transformations (test_transformation_api.py:152,236,311,454) and refresh (test_refresh_endpoint.py:61); jobs/knowledge/recipes/artifacts/chat suites use READ_WRITE fixtures exclusively, making the known WorkspaceRole-unenforced gap structurally invisible to the test suite.

issues.title: One status/catalog module (single source of world-state truth)
issues.summary: DESIGN-GATED (define the canonical shape). Status/catalog derived ~7 ways with user-visible divergence -> the #190 panic-loop input class. Single derivation for status; single table-catalog used by prompt+tools+API; write MATERIALIZING or delete it (15 readers, 0 writers); fix get_schema_status's dead shape; reconcile the 3 metadata read-scopes; fix fail-open dbt catalog.

findings.title: Status/catalog derivation implemented ~7 ways with concrete user-visible divergence (last_synced_at COMPLETED-only vs prompt/get_schema_status COMPLETED|PARTIAL); 'usable' view-schema predicate diverges during rebuild
findings.claim: Seven independent derivations of materialization/schema status exist (resume aggregate, jobs polling, refresh-status, workspace detail, two MCP tools, two prompt builders). Concretely, workspace detail's last_synced_at filters state=COMPLETED only, so a tenant whose runs always end PARTIAL shows last_synced_at:null in the UI while the agent prompt says 'Data is loaded and ready. Last updated: ...' from a COMPLETED|PARTIAL filter — same data, opposite story. The WorkspaceViewSchema 'usable' predicate also diverges: builders use PROVISIONING during rebuild, the query router accepts only ACTIVE, get_schema_status accepts ACTIVE|MATERIALIZING (a dead arm), so during any multi-tenant rebuild window get_schema_status says not_provisioned (inviting a redundant run_materialization) while the query tool says 'trigger a rebuild' though one is already running. The multi-tenant prompt also collapses vs.state==FAILED into the never-built branch, emitting 'No data has been loaded yet. Call run_materialization' and contradicting PR #229's get_schema_status fix.

findings.title: Multiple divergent table-catalog implementations: system prompt advertises terminal/transformation assets that list_tables omits; get_metadata returns 0 tables for multi-tenant; get_schema_status reads a dead result shape
findings.claim: Table truth is computed 4-6 ways that disagree, the contradictory-schema-response class behind the #190 panic loops. (1) transformation_aware_list_tables (prompt only) lists terminal stg_* TransformationAssets unconditionally with no physical-existence check, while the MCP list_tables tool uses plain pipeline_list_tables (raw names only; pipeline_config.dbt_models empty in all shipped YAMLs) — prompt advertises tables the agent's own tool omits/that failed dbt left absent. (2) get_metadata looks up TenantSchema by ctx.schema_name, which for multi-tenant is the ws_* view schema and never matches, returning success with table_count=0/tables={} while list_tables' view-schema branch works. (3) get_schema_status reads last_run.result['tables']/['table']+'rows_loaded' but the materializer writes only {sources,pipeline,transforms}, so it always returns tables=[] with state=active. (4) workspace_list_tables returns every view including stg_* and in-progress raw tables with no completion filtering, unlike the single-tenant catalog. No shared catalog service.

findings.title: get_schema_status reads an extinct result shape: single-tenant 'tables' always [] after a successful run
findings.claim: server.py reads last_run.result['tables'] or legacy result['table']+'rows_loaded', but run_pipeline/the materializer persist only {pipeline, sources, transforms} and top-level rows_loaded — neither branch matches, so single-tenant get_schema_status always returns exists=True/state=active/tables=[] even after a fully successful materialization, disagreeing with list_tables in the same conversation. Rename residue from the pre-pipeline result shape; mitigated because the prompt steers the agent to list_tables. (Distinct from the broader catalog-divergence cluster: this is the specific dead-key bug.)

findings.title: SchemaState.MATERIALIZING is never written by production code: ~15 readers branch on a dead state; single-tenant in-progress guard unreachable
findings.claim: No code anywhere in apps/ or mcp_server/ assigns MATERIALIZING (grep-verified; only test fixtures set it; provision() goes PROVISIONING->ACTIVE before any data loads), yet 8-15 readers filter/branch on it (context routing, prompt assembly, status derivation, availability checks, get_schema_status's ACTIVE|MATERIALIZING filter that mismatches load_workspace_context's ACTIVE-only aget). The single-tenant prompt branch 'materialization already in progress — do NOT trigger another one' can never fire, so during an in-flight single-tenant load the prompt says data is loaded but no tables yet and list_tables appends 'Run run_materialization', steering a second thread into a parallel dispatch (the only deterrent the concurrency finding lacked). The multi-tenant twin correctly checks MaterializationRun.ACTIVE_STATES; 'available' status is reported during DROP/recreate windows.

findings.title: Hardcoded 'commcare_sync' pipeline fallback in four places returns wrong-provider metadata silently; cascade duplicated 3-4x
findings.claim: Four call sites fall back to registry.get('commcare_sync') when pipeline resolution fails — including for OCS/Connect tenants and multi-tenant view schemas — silently using wrong-provider pipeline metadata instead of surfacing an error. The 'last run's pipeline -> provider pipeline -> commcare_sync' cascade is copy-pasted across views.py (twice), server.py, and a fourth variant in prompt assembly that skips the last-run step, so prompt and tools can name different pipelines for a tenant whose provider mapping changed. apps/workspaces and apps/agents also import mcp_server internals directly, making the 'standalone MCP server' boundary one-directional fiction.

findings.title: pipeline_list_tables is fail-closed for sources but fail-open for dbt models; transient DB error yields a phantom-table or empty catalog that instructs re-materialization
findings.claim: _live_tables_in_schema returns set() on any DB error (docstring promises empty-list-on-error to avoid phantom rows), and pipeline_list_tables correctly drops sources but then lists dbt models optimistically (`if live_table_names and ...`), so on a transient blip the catalog would contain only possibly-nonexistent dbt tables (NOT_FOUND streaks / #190 input shape) — currently moot only because all three pipeline YAMLs declare no transforms.models. When the empty-set path yields zero tables, list_tables decorates it with 'No completed materialization run found. Run run_materialization to load data' inside a success envelope, turning a DB blip into an agent instruction to launch a full expensive re-materialization of healthy data; the same helper backs the data-dictionary API (200s with {tables:{}}) and _get_all_columns returns {} on error.

findings.title: TenantMetadata is per-membership but read with three different scopes: column annotations appear/disappear per user and per surface
findings.claim: The materializer writes TenantMetadata only to the triggering user's membership (OneToOne to TenantMembership); the prompt reads user-scoped (returning None for users who never materialized -> no JSONB column annotations in their prompt), while MCP describe_table/get_metadata and the data dictionary read any membership's row (.afirst()/.first(), arbitrary). The same tenant/table yields different metadata by surface and by user.

issues.title: Credential lifetime for long jobs (CommCare 15-min OAuth TTL)
issues.summary: Any CommCare-OAuth materialization >15 min is structurally impossible today: one credential snapshot per run, no mid-run/401 refresh, stale-token fallback, no 'reconnect your account' mapping. Plus CommCare/OCS retry hardening, uncancellable Retry-After sleep, and refresh-revokes-running-token race.

findings.title: One credential snapshot per materialization run with no mid-run/401 refresh: CommCare's 15-min OAuth TTL makes long CommCare OAuth syncs structurally impossible
findings.chain: tasks.py:264 aresolve_credential once per tenant -> credential_resolver.py:102 refresh only if <5min buffer -> static dict into run_pipeline on asyncio.to_thread -> commcare_base.py:47 token baked into session headers -> 15-min TTL passes mid-load -> commcare_base.py:62-67 CommCareAuthError on 401 -> run FAILED/PARTIAL; non-resumable -> retry restarts from zero -> can never complete
findings.claim: aresolve_credential runs once per tenant at worker-task start (proactive refresh only if <5 min to expiry, token_refresh.py:19); the token string is frozen into requests.Session headers for the whole run_pipeline invocation with no mid-run or 401-reactive refresh (materializer.py:96-127 reuses the same dict for discover+every source; commcare_base.py:47 bakes it into session headers at loader __init__). Verified upstream TTLs: CommCare HQ 15 min, OCS 10h, Connect 2 weeks. Any CommCare-OAuth materialization exceeding ~15 min hits 401 mid-load and fails (commcare_base.py:62-67 raises CommCareAuthError); because CommCare sources are non-resumable every retry restarts from zero with another <=15-min token, so large CommCare OAuth domains can NEVER complete. OCS latent (10h vs run), Connect low tail risk softened by cursor resume. The start-of-run variant of this exact bug already bit in deployment (commit d4dee2e, #112, AUTH_TOKEN_EXPIRED) and was fixed only at resolution time. Distinct from the known 'Connect-only retry hardening' finding — this is token-lifetime vs run-duration across all OAuth providers.

findings.title: Refresh-failure falls back to a known-stale token and no 401 anywhere maps to re-authentication guidance; reactive-refresh path is dead code with false docstring
findings.chain: credential_resolver.py:107-111 TokenRefreshError -> logger.warning + return stale token -> provision+discover with doomed token -> loader 401 -> AuthError -> error_summary verbatim, no reauth guidance (tasks.py:1085-1125); separately token_refresh.py:3-5 docstring claims reactive refresh but only proactive callers exist; envelope.py:31 AUTH_TOKEN_EXPIRED producer-less
findings.claim: Two interlocking gaps on the credential seam: (1) credential_resolver.py:107-111 catches TokenRefreshError and proceeds with the existing token it just determined is within 5 min of expiry or already expired ('using existing token'), rather than failing closed like the OCS team-mismatch guard 20 lines up — the run provisions a schema and burns the discover phase before dying at the first authenticated request. No branch in _summarize_error, ThreadJob.error_summary, or any resume prompt maps a 401 to 'reconnect your account' (the only credential guidance is the missing-credential case, tasks.py:1103-1108), so expired/revoked/wrong-permission are indistinguishable to agent and user. (2) token_refresh.py:3-5 docstring claims the service is called 'reactively (after 401)', but refresh_oauth_token has exactly two callers — credential_resolver.py:106 (proactive, task-start) and auth_views.py:240 (interactive providers poll); every loader 401 raises a provider AuthError that no handler maps to refresh-and-retry. The AUTH_TOKEN_EXPIRED envelope code (mcp_server/envelope.py:31, added with the #112 fix) has zero producers — only a test references the constant. Fixed-where-it-bit residue.

findings.title: CommCare HQ actively rate-limits both APIs (429 + Retry-After by design); Scout's CommCare loaders have no retry and fail the run — raises the known Connect-only-hardening finding to expected-in-production for CommCare
findings.chain: materialize -> commcare_cases.py:51 self._get(url).json() -> commcare_base.py:61-69 raise_for_status() on 429 -> upstream hqcase/views.py:94 @api_throttle -> api/decorators.py:58 HttpResponse(status=429, Retry-After) -> HTTPError fails the run
findings.claim: Verified upstream: case_api (Case API v2) is decorated @api_throttle which returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header (corehq/apps/api/decorators.py:51-61), and the tastypie Forms v0.5 resource uses HQThrottle via CustomResourceMeta. Limits include a constant floor of per_second=1, per_minute=10, per_hour=30, per_day=50 plus per-user ratio scaling. Scout's CommCareBaseLoader._get does bare resp.raise_for_status() with no retry adapter and no Retry-After handling, so a designed throttle response becomes an unhandled HTTPError that fails the source/materialization run. Large-domain syncs (thousands of back-to-back page requests) are exactly the traffic this throttle polices. This RAISES the existing 'Retry/error-shape hardening applied only to Connect loaders' finding from latent to expected-in-production for CommCare; conversely OCS has no app-level throttle upstream, slightly lowering the OCS half.

findings.title: Connect Retry-After honored uncapped inside the single worker thread; '~14s worst case' comment false and the sleep is uncancellable
findings.chain: connect_base.py:61-69 Retry(respect_retry_after_header=True, 429 in forcelist) -> urllib3 2.6.3 Retry.sleep sleeps full header value uncapped, up to 3x/request -> runs inside _load_and_commit_source on the to_thread worker -> cancellation only checked between pages (tasks.py:485-494) -> sole worker slot held
findings.claim: connect_base.py sets respect_retry_after_header=True with 429/5xx in the forcelist; pinned urllib3 2.6.3's Retry.sleep sleeps the full server-supplied Retry-After uncapped (backoff_max bounds only the exponential path), up to 3 times per request, synchronously on the asyncio.to_thread pipeline thread. The comment at connect_base.py:31-32 promising '~14s worst case' is false whenever Retry-After is present. During the sleep the between-pages cancellation checkpoint (tasks.py:485-494) cannot fire, and on the single-worker deployment the platform's only background slot is held for the full sleep. Token-TTL interaction is minor for Connect's 2-week tokens but the worker-monopolization cost is real. Distinct from the known Connect retry-hardening finding (that one is about hardening absence; this is about an uncapped/uncancellable sleep blocking the sole worker).

findings.title: Concurrent interactive token refresh (providers poll) can revoke the access token a long-running load is using mid-run
findings.chain: auth_views.py:235-243 providers_view refresh on poll -> DOT revokes old access token (upstream behavior, unverified) -> running loader session holds revoked token -> next page request 401 -> AuthError -> run fails
