Independently, all 7 Connect writers commit DROP/CREATE up-front and commit per page regardless of the flag, violating _load_and_commit_source's documented single-transaction contract for non-resumable sources: a mid-load failure/cancel durably leaves a partial/empty table while the run records rows:0 — readable via the query tool (catalog hides only state!=completed). The constant's docstring is false and the dead resume plumbing/'Resumable:' docstrings invite re-enablement; replay tests use id-less fixtures so nothing would catch it.

issues.title: Reference-drift detection janitor (stored SQL/table refs)
issues.summary: No drift detection for any stored schema reference (artifact SQL, knowledge, learnings, recipes); every rename mechanism ships without migrating refs. Add a janitor that validates stored refs against live catalogs and flags rather than silently rotting.

findings.title: No drift detection for stored schema references anywhere; rename events (PR #228 prefix change, tenant-count transitions) silently invalidate artifacts/learnings/recipes/knowledge
findings.claim: Nothing validates or reconciles free-text schema references after any rename. Artifact source_queries are persisted verbatim with no table-existence check (the validator computes tables_accessed — an unused hook); KnowledgeRetriever injects all entries/annotations/top-20 learnings with zero liveness check; no janitor/cron/command inspects any reference store. Every name-changing mechanism ships without migrating references: PR #228 (2aaf4fb) changed the view prefix to head23_digest8 for every tenant whose sanitized canonical_name exceeds 32 chars (a 51-char prod tenant is named), and single<->multi tenant-count transitions flip the entire naming regime between t_<id> plain names and ws_* prefixed views — all pre-transition stored references are silently invalid, masked today only because the two replay surfaces (artifacts, recipes) are independently broken. The multi-tenant namespace prompt hint also misdescribes hashed prefixes as 'the tenant name'.

issues.title: LangGraph checkpoint retention + deletion on member removal
issues.summary: Member removal deletes Thread rows but never LangGraph checkpoints; checkpoints are never pruned anywhere (retention/privacy gap + unbounded growth).

findings.title: Member removal deletes Thread rows but never deletes LangGraph checkpoints; checkpoints never pruned anywhere
findings.claim: WorkspaceMemberDetailView.delete (and workspace deletion cascade) removes the member's Thread rows, but nothing ever deletes the corresponding LangGraph checkpoints — full conversation contents including query results persist indefinitely in the checkpoint tables after membership removal (data-retention/privacy gap), checkpoints accrete unboundedly per superstep (a K-tool turn writes ~2K+1 full-history copies, ~99% write-only), and a re-added member POSTing the old thread UUID gets a fresh Thread row whose checkpointer silently resumes the old conversation. No thread-delete endpoint and no checkpoint-deletion call site exists anywhere; ownership cleanup is also inconsistent (threads destroyed while the member's artifacts/recipes/knowledge survive). Unbounded platform-DB growth on the same RDS as state tables and the queue.

issues.title: Dead code / rename residue / cosmetic drift cleanup
issues.summary: Dead DRF permission classes, dual checkpointer module (+ MemorySaver-in-prod footgun), project_id audit residue, domainSlice naming stratum, export 501, dead share surface, vestigial RecipeStep, execute_async; stale Celery docstrings, removed-model prompt sections, doubled heading, inline imports; TS type lies; minor run-lifecycle drift; the two REFUTED findings (kept for the record).

findings.title: Large dead-code / rename-residue cluster (permissions, dual checkpointer, project_id audit, domainSlice, export 501, dead share surface, legacy data_dictionary, RecipeStep, execute_async, etc.)
findings.claim: Multiple abandoned ~80% passes remain live-looking: dead DRF permission classes (covered separately); apps/agents/memory/checkpointer.py get_postgres_checkpointer/get_sync_checkpointer are dead with a silent MemorySaver-in-production fallback footgun while the live checkpointer is apps/chat (only get_database_url is imported); stream.py audit-logs input_state['project_id'] which no longer exists (always empty) and is emitted at INFO that production root-WARNING suppresses entirely; frontend still uses domainSlice/activeDomainId/setActiveDomainByTenantId no-op and a TenantMembership legacy alias with phantom fields; ArtifactExportView returns 501 for PNG/PDF while export.py has unused playwright implementations (playwright not in pyproject); RecipeStep model+admin+~150 test lines and RecipeRunner.execute_async have zero production readers; Workspace.data_dictionary has no writers so TableDetailView's legacy fallback and learning_tool's table validation are permanent no-ops; Artifact.create_new_version, run_commcare_sync shim, execute_internal_query, frontend dropdown-menu/barrels/STARTER_QUESTIONS are dead; CLAUDE.md/TODO.md misstate features (golden queries/eval runs absent; SET ROLE isolation listed unimplemented though query.py does it).

findings.title: Cosmetic drift cluster: stale Celery docstrings, removed-model prompt sections, doubled Knowledge Base heading, inline imports, three-strata naming residue
findings.claim: RefreshSchemaView/create_refresh_schema docstrings still say 'Celery task' 13 months after the Procrastinate migration; BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT carries a CRITICAL 'Canonical Metrics' section for a model replaced by generic KnowledgeEntry; _build_system_prompt wraps retriever output in '## Knowledge Base' while the retriever emits its own, doubling the heading in every prompt with entries; DataDictionaryView's docstring states the wrong route/'latest completed' (also accepts PARTIAL); inline imports at base.py:244,261 violate the project import policy (27897fc sweep missed them); TODO.md lists SET ROLE isolation as unimplemented though query.py implements it; assorted false docstrings (user_role 'controls access', setCachedUserTenants cache invariant, mcp auth token injection, question-blind retriever, completed_modules INTEGER-vs-TEXT, _RESUMABLE_CONNECT_SOURCES comment, _load_and_commit_source atomicity); _json_safe Decimal-to-float coercion is dead; CommCareCaseLoader legacy access_token kwarg kept alive only by its own tests; DEPLOYMENT.md drift inventory (Redis 'Celery broker', nginx '/mcp/' location, scout/mcp ECR image, pre-deploy hook all stale; pre-connect hook contradicts the documented SSH setup); frontend store still speaks 'domain' vs API 'workspace' vs UI 'data source'; the frontend-layout rule contradicts the pages it cites as exemplars.

findings.title: Minor type lies and cosmetic seam drift: TS contracts stricter than runtime payloads, per-process prompt-cache key omits schema/knowledge state, list-repr in recipe results
findings.claim: Several hand-written TS contracts are stricter than the runtime payload (Recipe[] list items lack required prompt/variables; ArtifactPanel QueryResult.sql required but error entries carry only {name,error}; workspaceApi.create omits schema_status/last_synced_at required by WorkspaceListItem; created_by_name returned but never rendered) — none break today but each traps the next consumer. The system-prompt cache key omits schema/knowledge state and the cache is per-process, so a freshly-materialized workspace can serve a 'No data loaded yet' prompt for up to 60s in the API process; runner._extract_response_content does str(msg.content) producing a Python list-repr for content blocks; error-correlation is inconsistent (one endpoint mints refs, the stream path none, no cross-process correlation id); get_schema_status carries a third legacy {table,rows_loaded} spelling; live SSE empty tool-input vs reload real args. A large one-sided dead-surface inventory (refresh/status, transformations frontend, tenants/select, theme param, providers connected boolean) also exists.

findings.title: Minor run-lifecycle drift: terminal-run progress writes, unconditional result clobber, post-COMPLETED step-guard raise; materialized_row_count wrong after resumed run
findings.claim: progress_updater writes progress unconditionally onto terminal runs; the cancel-before-transform branch unconditionally overwrites result; the step-count guard raises AFTER the run is marked COMPLETED and the schema ACTIVE (a drift would report tenant failure while the row says COMPLETED); cancel_materialization's docstring documents the FAILED drift as intended. On resume the visits writer restarts total=0 so the completion entry records only this run's increment (catalog reports e.g. 10k for a 60k-row table via materialized_row_count, repeated in status summaries; mitigated only by row_count_verified:false), and visits resume re-mixes mutable status/review_status fields the ON CONFLICT exists to refresh without re-fetching behind the watermark.

findings.title: DEBUG MemorySaver fallback cached for process lifetime; dead memory checkpointer module carries a silent prod MemorySaver fallback
findings.claim: apps/chat/checkpointer.py correctly raises in production when Postgres is unavailable, but under DEBUG one startup blip caches MemorySaver in the module global, silently routing all subsequent conversations to process memory until restart. The parallel apps/agents/memory/checkpointer.py get_postgres_checkpointer falls back to MemorySaver on any failure ('Conversations will NOT be persisted') and get_sync_checkpointer always returns MemorySaver; both are exported but unused (only get_database_url is imported) — a future caller picking the obvious memory module would reintroduce the silent history-loss the live module was hardened against. The memory module also resolves the DB env-first (raw DATABASE_URL, then a DB_HOST/DB_NAME convention used nowhere else) with Django settings third, two sources of truth that diverge under PgBouncer/CI.

findings.title: auto_create_workspace_on_membership performs three writes outside a transaction
findings.claim: The post_save signal creates Workspace, then WorkspaceTenant, then WorkspaceMembership with no transaction.atomic(); a failure between steps leaves an orphan auto-workspace with no tenant and/or no membership, which load_workspace_context later rejects with 'Workspace has no tenants'. The idempotency guard is sound, so this is a durability gap rather than a duplication race. Fires on every first-time TenantMembership creation.

findings.title: Connect resumable writers can duplicate on stale-cursor replay; safety rests on an implicit invariant that the zombie-janitor fix would break
findings.claim: Five resumable Connect tables (completed_works, payments, invoices, assessments, completed_modules) have surrogate IDENTITY PKs and no ON CONFLICT, so any page replay re-inserts silently. Replay is currently prevented only because hard-crashed runs stay LOADING (never PARTIAL/FAILED), so _load_prior_resume_cursors forces a clean DROP+reload; cursor_state is persisted per page after commit and lags committed rows. Adding the recommended zombie-run janitor without nulling cursor_state, or any future out-of-process FAILED flip, creates a real duplication path. Becomes reachable if the zombie-janitor fix lands naively.

issues.title: [DESIGN] Content-satellite redesign: recipes + knowledge + artifacts
issues.summary: The wave-1/2 fixes restore recipes/knowledge/artifacts to working-AS-DESIGNED. Whether that current design is what we want is a separate product question. Brainstorm -> spec before committing to repair-in-place vs rethink. References recipe-runner-fix, knowledge-fixes, artifact-multitenant-render, artifact-sandbox-isolation, permission-layer (recipe privacy).

issues.title: [POLICY] Sibling-sweep as fix policy on every incident-fix PR
issues.summary: Pattern 1 ('fixed-where-it-bit') is the single most predictive finding generator: every fix PR must list the grep for sibling sites and either fix or explicitly tick them off.

issues.title: [POLICY] Re-run the arch-review coverage matrix quarterly
issues.summary: The methodology is repeatable; diff the next findings DB against this run (2026-06-12) to measure remediation and catch drift.
